title: chenopodium desertorum subsp. microphyllum
intro: Chenopodium desertorum subsp. microphyllum, also known as a subspecies of the desert spinach or pitseed goosefoot, is a type of plant belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. The medicinal properties of Chenopodium desertorum subsp. microphyllum include its ability to reduce inflammation and exhibit antioxidant activity. This plant has been used for its health benefits, such as treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Chenopodium desertorum subsp. microphyllum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or topical application.
